title: Cut shapes into equal parts
description: Practice telling if a shape has been divided into equal parts.

assessment_items.question_content:
This cake is cut into $6$ pieces.



[[☃ image 1]]

**Is each piece equal to $\dfrac16$ of the area of the cake?**  
*It's okay if each slice has different toppings.*
  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

Some pieces have larger area than others.  



[[☃ image 1]]
Because the whole is $\red{\text{not}}$ divided into $\green6$ equal parts, each part is $\red{\text{not}}$ $\dfrac{1}{\green6}$ of the whole.
No, each piece is not equal to $\dfrac{1}{\green6}$ of the cake.

options.alt: A cake is cut into two large slices and four smaller slices.

choices.content: Yes
No

assessment_items.question_content:
This pizza is cut into $8$ pieces.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Is each piece equal to $\dfrac18$ of the area of the pizza?**  
*It's okay if each slice has different toppings.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

Some pieces have larger area than others.  

[[☃ image 1]]
Because the whole is $\red{\text{not}}$ divided into $\green8$ equal parts, each part is $\red{\text{not}}$ $\dfrac{1}{\green8}$ of the whole.
No, each piece is not equal to $\dfrac{1}{\green8}$ of the pizza.

options.alt: A pizza is cut into eight pieces of different sizes.

choices.content: Yes
No

assessment_items.question_content:
This pie is cut into $4$ pieces.


[[☃ image 1]]

**Is each piece equal to $\dfrac14$ of the area of the pie?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

Some pieces have larger area than others.  

[[☃ image 1]]
Because the whole is $\red{\text{not}}$ divided into $\green4$ equal parts, each part is $\red{\text{not}}$ $\dfrac{1}{\green4}$ of the whole.
No, each piece is not equal to $\dfrac{1}{\green4}$ of the pie.

options.alt: A pie is shown. It has been cut into 4 pieces. Some pieces are larger than others.

choices.content: Yes
No

assessment_items.question_content:
This pie is cut into $2$ pieces.


[[☃ image 1]]

**Is each piece equal to $\dfrac12$ of the area of the pie?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

Some pieces have larger area than others.  


[[☃ image 1]]
Because the whole is $\redD{\text{not}}$ divided into $\greenD{2}$ equal parts, each part is $\redD{\text{not}}$ $\dfrac{1}{\greenD{2}}$ of the whole.
No, each piece is not equal to $\dfrac{1}{\greenD{2}}$ of the pie.

options.alt: A pie cut into two unequal pieces.

choices.content: Yes
No

assessment_items.question_content:
This cookie is cut into $3$ pieces.


[[☃ image 1]]

**Is each piece equal to $\dfrac13$ of the area of the cookie?**  
*It's okay if each piece has different toppings.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

Some pieces have larger area than others.  


[[☃ image 1]]
Because the whole is $\red{\text{not}}$ divided into $\green3$ equal parts, each part is $\red{\text{not}}$ $\dfrac{1}{\green3}$ of the whole.
No, each piece is not equal to $\dfrac{1}{\green3}$ of the cookie.

options.alt: A cookie cut into three pieces of different sizes.

choices.content: Yes
No

assessment_items.question_content:
This pizza is cut into $2$ pieces.


[[☃ image 1]]

**Is each piece equal to $\dfrac12$ of the area of the pizza?**  
*It's okay if each slice has different toppings.*


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.
Both pieces have the same area.
When we divide a whole into $\green2$ equal parts, each part is $\dfrac{1}{\green2}$ of the whole.
Yes, each piece is equal to $\dfrac{1}{\green2}$ of the pizza.

options.alt: A pizza is cut into two equal pieces.

choices.content: Yes
No

assessment_items.question_content:
This pie is cut into $3$ pieces.



[[☃ image 1]]

**Is each piece equal to $\dfrac13$ of the area of the pie?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.
All of the pieces have the same area.
When we divide a whole into $\green3$ equal parts, each part is $\dfrac{1}{\green3}$ of the whole.
Yes, each piece is equal to $\dfrac{1}{\green3}$ of the pie.

options.alt: A pie cut into three pieces of the same size.

choices.content: Yes
No

choices.content: Yes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which figures are divided into equal parts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.
In these figures, some parts have larger area than others:

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

These figures are divided into equal parts:  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which rectangles have $\dfrac12$ of their area shaded?**   


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

The rectangle must be divided into $\blue{2 \text{ equal-sized}}$ parts.  

[[☃ image 1]]

 
[[☃ image 2]]
$\red1$ of the $\blue{2 \text{ equal-sized}}$ parts must be shaded.

These rectangles have $\dfrac12$ of their area shaded:


[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ image 2]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which circles have $\dfrac14$ of their area shaded?**   


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

The circle must be divided into $\blue{4 \text{ equal-sized}}$ parts.  

[[☃ image 1]]
$\red1$ of the $\blue{4 \text{ equal-sized}}$ parts must be shaded.

This circle has $\dfrac14$ of its area shaded:


[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A circle divided into four equal parts with one part shaded.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ed810f89cd09e24a46503461acce64af.png)
![A circle divided into four parts of different sizes with one part shaded.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6e917c9e6cf9a47797f832d710ef13cc.png)
![A circle divided into three equal parts with one part shaded.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/84c075555ffbb63b4212fbc900441a7f.png)
![A circle divided into four parts of different sizes with one part shaded.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fee5f63501b097b5f87bd904bd61b8cb.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which rectangles have $\dfrac16$ of their area shaded?**   

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

The rectangle must be divided into $\blueD{6 \text{ equal-sized}}$ parts.  

[[☃ image 1]]
$\redD1$ of the $\blueD{6 \text{ equal-sized}}$ parts must be shaded.

This rectangle has $\dfrac16$ of its area shaded:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A rectangle is divided into 6 equal-sized parts. The third part is shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/566e1b91cd18bb20e998a9c9296b68140736090d)
![A rectangle is divided into 4 equal-sized parts. The first part is shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/25e6cca18b7c63b6a95ef1f385f8cbd200256033)

![A rectangle is divided into 6 parts of two different sizes. The first and fifth parts are the same size. The other four parts are the same size. The fifth part is shaded. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/92de782d42f6af08517f177140bbbb4a402191f7)
![A rectangle is divided into 5 equal-sized parts. The second part is shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/297565325542a12bc7cea2399b8a2d943c60839f)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which squares have $\dfrac16$ of their area shaded?**   

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Figures are divided into equal parts if all of their parts are the same size.

The square must be divided into $\blueD{6 \text{ equal-sized}}$ parts.  


[[☃ image 1]]

 
 

[[☃ image 2]]
$\redD1$ of the $\blueD{6 \text{ equal-sized}}$ parts must be shaded.

These squares have $\dfrac16$ of their area shaded:


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

![A square divided into two large triangles and four smaller triangles with one large triangle shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b704eee353b8d1bed91bdc1a71217879254d13a)
